text: This article explores the role of community nursing in Ecuador. Community nursing is a cornerstone of the Ecuadorian public health system and is particularly important in rural and disadvantaged urban areas for the promotion, prevention and control of disease. The study shows that community nurses work closely together with other health professionals, not only to deliver education but also to conduct national vaccination campaigns, to manage chronic conditions and to work together with patients to strengthen their self-care practices. The study highlights how education has had a positive effect, resulting in a significant reduction in stunting and in cases of diarrheal disease. It has also improved access to immunization in disadvantaged regions and improved adherence to treatments among patients with non-communicable diseases, thereby contributing to an improvement in quality of life. Community nurses in Ecuador, however, are facing a number of challenges, including lack of infrastructure, insufficient human and material resources, and access to ongoing training. Therefore, the article highlights the need for digital resources to support community nurses, adequate public policies and intersectoral collaboration to reinforce community nursing in Ecuador, with the ultimate goal of ensuring the optimal application of interventions and a more equable distribution of benefits at the national level.
